title: androsace elongata subsp. breistrofferi
intro: Androsace elongata subsp. breistrofferi is a rare, alpine plant species of the Primulaceae family, also known as the "Pyrenean rock jasmine" or "Breistroffer's rock jasmine", native to the high-altitude regions of the Pyrenees mountains. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Androsace elongata subsp. breistrofferi, such as infusions and tinctures, are sometimes used to treat minor skin irritations and wounds, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
